page: Microsoft Azure E16_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8171M processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Additionally, it includes 410 GB of local storage, while lacking dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by a tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the middle 50% range, with read-only large block speeds reaching 71.84 GB/sec and write speeds at 44.38 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16_v3 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high single-threaded CPU performance. It is well-suited for relational database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and moderate data processing tasks. Conversely, it is less optimal for single-threaded compression tasks or complex large-scale LLM inference due to its lower single-core performance.
description: Microsoft Azure E16_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and 410 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks highlight a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and text processing are positioned in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations achieve average, mid-tier results. Qualitatively, the server offers high memory density per vCPU, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute power. It is best suited for database management, caching systems like Redis, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 410 GB of local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, suited for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and local storage for database operations and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
410 GB of local storage included without dedicated GPU resources
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance despite weak single-core metrics
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound applications
